an1.333:1.1: "*Bhikkhus*, just as there is little in this Jambudīpa that is delightful in parks, delightful in forests, delightful in land, delightful in lotus ponds;
an1.333:1.2: but far more of it is rough and uneven, with difficult river crossings, regions of stumps and thorns, and rugged mountains —
an1.333:1.3: so too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings born on land, while far more are those born in water.
an1.334:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings reborn among humans;
an1.334:1.2: far more are those reborn elsewhere than among humans.
an1.335:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings reborn in the central lands;
an1.335:1.2: far more are those reborn in the borderlands, among those who do not know, among the barbarians.
an1.336:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who are wise, not dull, not mute, capable of discerning the meaning of what is well spoken and poorly spoken;
an1.336:1.2: far more are those who are lacking in *paññā*, dull, mute, incapable of discerning the meaning of what is well spoken and poorly spoken.
an1.337:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings endowed with the noble eye of *paññā*;
an1.337:1.2: far more are those gone into blindness [*avijjā*], bewildered.
an1.338:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who get to see the Tathāgata;
an1.338:1.2: far more are those who do not get to see the Tathāgata.
an1.339:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who get to hear the *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] proclaimed by the Tathāgata;
an1.339:1.2: far more are those who do not get to hear the *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] proclaimed by the Tathāgata.
an1.340:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having heard the *dhamma*, retain it;
an1.340:1.2: far more are those who, having heard the *dhamma*, do not retain it.
an1.341:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who examine the meaning of the *dhammas* they have retained;
an1.341:1.2: far more are those who do not examine the meaning of the *dhammas* they have retained.
an1.342:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having understood the meaning and understood the *dhamma*, practice in accordance with the *dhamma*;
an1.342:1.2: far more are those who, having understood the meaning and understood the *dhamma*, do not practice in accordance with the *dhamma*.
an1.343:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who feel stirred at things that call for stirring;
an1.343:1.2: far more are those who do not feel stirred at things that call for stirring.
an1.344:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, stirred, strive with appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*];
an1.344:1.2: far more are those who, stirred, do not strive with appropriate attention [*yoniso manasikāra*].
an1.345:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, making letting-go their object, attain *samādhi*, attain singleness of mind [*cittassekaggataṁ*];
an1.345:1.2: far more are those who, making letting-go their object, do not attain *samādhi*, do not attain singleness of mind [*cittassekaggataṁ*].
an1.346:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who obtain the finest food and finest flavors;
an1.346:1.2: far more are those who do not obtain the finest food and finest flavors, and survive by gleaning and by begging bowl.
an1.347:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who obtain the taste of meaning, the taste of *dhamma*, the taste of liberation;
an1.347:1.2: far more are those who do not obtain the taste of meaning, the taste of *dhamma*, the taste of liberation.
an1.347:1.3: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, you should train yourselves thus:
an1.347:1.4: 'We will be those who obtain the taste of meaning, the taste of *dhamma*, the taste of liberation.'
an1.347:1.5: This is how you should train yourselves, *bhikkhus*."
an1.348-350:1.1: "*Bhikkhus*, just as there is little in this Jambudīpa that is delightful in parks, delightful in forests, delightful in land, delightful in lotus ponds;
an1.348-350:1.2: but far more of it is rough and uneven, with difficult river crossings, regions of stumps and thorns, and rugged mountains —
an1.348-350:1.3: so too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away as humans, are reborn among humans; far more are those who, having passed away as humans, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts."
an1.351-353:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away as humans, are reborn among the gods;
an1.351-353:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away as humans, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.354-356:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away as gods, are reborn among the gods;
an1.354-356:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away as gods, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.357-359:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away as gods, are reborn among humans;
an1.357-359:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away as gods, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.360-362:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away from hell, are reborn among humans;
an1.360-362:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away from hell, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.363-365:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away from hell, are reborn among the gods;
an1.363-365:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away from hell, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.366-368:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away from the animal realm, are reborn among humans;
an1.366-368:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away from the animal realm, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.369-371:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away from the animal realm, are reborn among the gods;
an1.369-371:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away from the animal realm, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.372-374:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away from the realm of ghosts, are reborn among humans;
an1.372-374:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away from the realm of ghosts, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts.
an1.375-377:1.1: … So too, *bhikkhus*, few are the beings who, having passed away from the realm of ghosts, are reborn among the gods;
an1.375-377:1.2: far more are those who, having passed away from the realm of ghosts, are reborn in hell … reborn in the animal realm … reborn in the realm of ghosts."
